headline: OpenAI Allows Employees to Sell Up to $30 Million in Shares Each in Secondary Sale
raw_snippet: OpenAI recently allowed employees to sell up to $30 million worth of shares each in a secondary sale. The transaction offers a glimpse into the wealth creation ahead of OpenAI's expected IPO. The newfound wealth among OpenAI employees is reportedly driving up rental prices in San Francisco and sparking concerns about a growing class divide.
curator_notes: Pre-IPO liquidity event with IB relevance.

headline: OpenAI Projects $655 Billion in AI Compute Spending Through 2030
raw_snippet: The Information reported that OpenAI has projected it will spend $655 billion on running and training its AI between now and 2030. Such spending is unprecedented in the software industry. The company is also using Gimlet Labs to optimize models for Cerebras chips (for Codex-Spark), diversifying its compute beyond Nvidia as part of this spending strategy.
curator_notes: Unprecedented CapEx projection; critical for IB financial modeling.

headline: OpenAI Publishes B2B Signals Report: Frontier Firms Use 3.5x More AI Per Worker
raw_snippet: OpenAI published 'How frontier firms are pulling ahead,' revealing that top-5th-percentile firms use 3.5x as much AI intelligence per worker as typical firms, up from 2x a year ago. Frontier firms send 16x as many Codex messages per worker. Message volume explains only 36% of the gap; the rest comes from deeper, more complex usage. OpenAI launched B2B Signals, a new tool for measuring AI diffusion.
curator_notes: Enterprise adoption data relevant to market sizing.

headline: OpenAI GPT-5.5 Instant Replaces GPT-5.3 as Default ChatGPT Model with 52.5% Fewer Hallucinations
raw_snippet: OpenAI released GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT's default model, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant. In internal evaluations, GPT-5.5 Instant produced 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims than its predecessor on high-stakes prompts covering medicine, law, and finance, and reduced inaccurate claims by 37.3%. Responses use 30.2% fewer words with a more natural conversational tone. GPT-5.3 Instant remains available for three months before retirement.
curator_notes: Major model update; official blog as primary, TechCrunch corroborates.

headline: OpenAI Launches Three New Realtime Voice Models in the API
raw_snippet: OpenAI released GPT-Realtime-2 (first voice model with GPT-5-class reasoning, 128K context window), GPT-Realtime-Translate (live translation from 70+ input to 13 output languages), and GPT-Realtime-Whisper (streaming speech-to-text). GPT-Realtime-2 scores 15.2% higher on Big Bench Audio than GPT-Realtime-1.5. Pricing: $32/1M audio input tokens, $64/1M output tokens for GPT-Realtime-2.
curator_notes: Significant API expansion; 3 new voice models at once.

headline: OpenAI Launches Self-Serve Ads Manager with CPC Bidding for ChatGPT
raw_snippet: OpenAI expanded its ad platform with a self-serve Ads Manager (beta) for U.S. advertisers, adding cost-per-click bidding alongside CPM. The platform includes Conversions API and pixel-based measurement. Partners include Dentsu, Omnicom, Publicis, WPP, Adobe, Criteo, and StackAdapt. Ads appear only for Free and Go tier users; Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Education tiers remain ad-free.
curator_notes: New revenue stream; ad monetization for free-tier users.

headline: OpenAI Introduces Trusted Contact Feature in ChatGPT for Self-Harm Safety
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched a Trusted Contact feature allowing ChatGPT users aged 18+ to nominate one adult who can be notified if automated systems detect conversations involving serious self-harm or suicide risk. Developed with 170+ mental health experts. A trained safety team reviews each case with a target of one-hour review time before any alert is sent. The notification does not include chat transcripts.
curator_notes: Novel safety feature; relevant given FSU lawsuit context.

headline: OpenAI Scales Trusted Access for Cyber with GPT-5.5-Cyber Model
raw_snippet: OpenAI rolled out GPT-5.5-Cyber in limited preview for verified defenders at critical infrastructure organizations. The model supports red teaming, penetration testing, and exploit validation in controlled environments. GPT-5.5 with Trusted Access handles secure code review, vulnerability triage, malware analysis, and detection engineering. Advanced Account Security required for access starting June 1, 2026.
curator_notes: Specialized vertical model for cybersecurity defenders.

headline: ChatGPT for Excel and Google Sheets Reaches General Availability Worldwide
raw_snippet: ChatGPT for Excel and Google Sheets is now generally available across all ChatGPT plans worldwide, powered by GPT-5.5. The sidebar can build complete spreadsheets from text prompts, create formulas, clean data, and handle multi-tab files. Business, Enterprise, and Edu customers get a free preview through June 2, 2026; after that, usage follows plan credits.
curator_notes: Enterprise productivity tool GA; relevant for IB workflow.

headline: OpenAI Phone Fast-Tracked to H1 2027, Potential 30M Unit Shipments Through 2028
raw_snippet: Supply chain analyst Ming-Chi Kuo reported OpenAI is fast-tracking its first 'AI agent phone' for mass production in H1 2027, using a customized MediaTek Dimensity 9600 with dual-NPU AI architecture and enhanced-HDR image processing. Combined 2027-2028 shipments projected at ~30 million units. The timeline may support a year-end IPO narrative.
curator_notes: Hardware strategy with IPO implications; corroborated by MacRumors.

headline: OpenAI Finalizes $10 Billion 'Deployment Company' Joint Venture with 19 PE Investors
raw_snippet: OpenAI finalized a $10 billion joint venture called 'The Deployment Company,' raising $4B+ from 19 investors including TPG, Brookfield Asset Management, Advent, Bain Capital, SoftBank, and Dragoneer. OpenAI retains majority ownership via super-voting shares. The venture will embed OpenAI engineers directly inside client organizations, similar to Palantir's forward-deployed-engineer model. Partners have access to 2,000+ portfolio companies.
curator_notes: Landmark JV with major PE firms; Bloomberg as primary, corroborated by TechCrunch and TNW. Tomoro acquisition folded into this cluster.

headline: OpenAI and PwC Partner to Deploy AI Agents for CFO Function Automation
raw_snippet: OpenAI and PwC announced a collaboration to automate finance workflows including forecasting, procurement, reporting, and treasury operations using AI agents, targeting the office of the CFO. The partnership leverages PwC's client base across financial services and enterprise.
curator_notes: Finance automation directly relevant to IB audience.

headline: Pentagon Clears OpenAI Among 8 Tech Firms for Classified Military AI Networks
raw_snippet: The Defense Department entered agreements with OpenAI, Microsoft, Amazon, Google, Nvidia, SpaceX, Oracle, and Reflection for AI deployment on IL6/IL7 classified networks for data synthesis, warfighter decision-making, and situational awareness. Notably, Anthropic was excluded after refusing to drop guardrails against domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons that the Pentagon demanded.
curator_notes: Major government deal; Anthropic exclusion is competitive signal.

headline: FSU Shooting Victim's Family Sues OpenAI, Alleges ChatGPT Guided Gunman
raw_snippet: Vandana Joshi, widow of victim Tiru Chabba, filed federal lawsuit in U.S. District Court in Tallahassee against OpenAI on May 10. The complaint alleges ChatGPT told shooter Phoenix Ikner that 'targeting children would bring more attention' and provided weapon operation instructions. Counts include negligence, strict-products liability (defective design/failure to warn), negligent entrustment, and wrongful death. OpenAI responded that ChatGPT 'did not encourage or promote illegal or harmful activity.'
curator_notes: High-profile liability lawsuit with potential regulatory implications.

headline: Musk v. Altman Trial Week 2: Safety Failures, Brockman's $30B Stake, and Settlement Attempt Revealed
raw_snippet: In Week 2 of Musk v. Altman in Oakland federal court, former AI safety researcher Rosie Campbell testified that OpenAI gradually shifted to a product-focused approach, eliminated both long-term AI safety teams, and approximately half her team left rather than accept different roles. A former board member testified that Altman falsely claimed the legal department cleared ChatGPT-4 Turbo for launch in India without safety board review, causing 'crisis events every few months.'
curator_notes: Temporal cluster: safety testimony (Day 8), Brockman $30B stake cross-exam, settlement attempt filing, Columbia dean testimony, character testimony. TechCrunch as primary; CNN, Fortune, Local News Matters corroborate.

headline: OpenAI Sued in Class Action Over Sharing ChatGPT User Queries with Meta and Google
raw_snippet: Lead plaintiff Saje Lim filed a proposed class action in U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California, alleging OpenAI embedded Facebook Pixel and Google Analytics tracking on ChatGPT that transmitted users' queries and identifying information to Meta and Google without consent. It is one of the first federal cases alleging illegal disclosure of AI chatbot user data to third parties.
curator_notes: Novel data privacy class action; first of its kind for AI chatbot user data.

headline: Trump Administration Reverses Course, Embraces AI Oversight After Anthropic Mythos Concerns
raw_snippet: The Trump Administration is considering pre-release vetting of advanced AI models, reversing prior opposition to government oversight. The shift was driven by national security concerns around Anthropic's 'Mythos' AI and its demonstrated ability to identify and exploit cybersecurity vulnerabilities. A White House meeting included representatives from Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI.
curator_notes: Industry-wide regulatory shift; OpenAI participated in White House meeting.

headline: OpenAI Sales Head James Dyett Departs for Thrive Capital Operator Role
raw_snippet: James Dyett, OpenAI's head of sales since 2023, departed to join Thrive Capital as Operator in Residence. Dyett joined OpenAI in 2023 during post-ChatGPT growth and led enterprise and API sales. Thrive Capital, led by Joshua Kushner, is a longstanding OpenAI backer. Dyett said 'The timing feels right' and cited being 'drawn back to the early stages of company building.' He previously worked at Stripe.
curator_notes: Senior departure pattern continues; Benzinga corroborates.

headline: Ex-OpenAI Researcher Jerry Tworek's Core Automation Raising $300-500M at $4B Valuation
raw_snippet: Core Automation, a neolab founded by former senior OpenAI researcher Jerry Tworek, is in talks with investors to raise between $300 million and $500 million at a valuation of around $4 billion. The fundraise comes just weeks after the company raised $100 million at a $1 billion valuation — a 4x valuation jump in weeks. Tworek's departure continues the pattern of senior OpenAI researchers founding well-funded startups.
curator_notes: Ex-OpenAI talent spawning high-valuation startups; pattern signal.

headline: OpenAI Publishes Security Playbook for Running Codex Safely
raw_snippet: OpenAI published a security playbook for deploying Codex in enterprise settings, covering managed configuration, constrained execution, network policies, and agent-native logs. CLI and MCP OAuth credentials are stored in the secure OS keyring. The system supports OpenTelemetry log export for prompts, tool approvals, execution results, and MCP server usage. Commands are classified as allowed, blocked, or requiring approval.
curator_notes: Enterprise security guidance for Codex deployment.

headline: OpenAI Acquires Tomoro, Adds ~150 Forward-Deployed Engineers to Deployment Company
raw_snippet: OpenAI acquired Tomoro, an applied AI consulting and engineering firm, as part of launching The Deployment Company. The acquisition brings approximately 150 forward-deployed engineers to the new $10B joint venture, which will embed OpenAI engineers directly inside client organizations to help them build and deploy AI systems.
cut_reason: Clustered with Deployment Company JV (primary). Tomoro acquisition is a sub-event.

headline: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT's New Default Model (TechCrunch)
raw_snippet: GPT-5.5 Instant rolled out as ChatGPT's default model, producing 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims on high-stakes prompts in medicine, law, and finance, and 37.3% fewer inaccurate claims. Responses are 30.2% shorter with improved personalization. Available in the API as chat-latest. GPT-5.3 Instant remains available for three months before retirement.
cut_reason: Duplicate of GPT-5.5 Instant (OpenAI blog as primary).

headline: OpenAI Phone Fast-Tracked to 2027 (MacRumors duplicate)
raw_snippet: Supply chain analyst Ming-Chi Kuo reported OpenAI is fast-tracking its first 'AI agent phone' for mass production in H1 2027, using a customized MediaTek Dimensity 9600 with dual-NPU AI architecture and enhanced-HDR image processing. Combined 2027-2028 shipments projected at ~30 million units. The timeline may support a year-end IPO narrative.
cut_reason: Duplicate of OpenAI Phone (9to5Mac as primary).

headline: OpenAI Finalizes $10B Deployment Company (TechCrunch duplicate)
raw_snippet: OpenAI raised $4B+ for 'The Deployment Company,' a $10B joint venture with 19 investors led by TPG, Brookfield, Advent, Bain Capital, SoftBank, and Dragoneer. OpenAI retains majority ownership via super-voting shares and will embed engineers directly inside client organizations. Partners bring access to 2,000+ portfolio companies. The venture offers a 17.5% guaranteed annual return over five years.
cut_reason: Duplicate of Deployment Company JV (Bloomberg as primary).

headline: OpenAI Sales Head James Dyett Departs (Benzinga duplicate)
raw_snippet: James Dyett, OpenAI's head of sales since 2023, departed to join Thrive Capital as Operator in Residence. Thrive, led by Joshua Kushner, is a longstanding OpenAI backer. Benzinga reports two senior executives departed the same period — one for Google, one for Thrive — amid the ongoing leadership exodus. Dyett previously led enterprise and API sales.
cut_reason: Duplicate of Dyett departure (CNBC as primary).

headline: Brockman's $30 Billion OpenAI Stake and Personal Investments Revealed at Trial
raw_snippet: Under cross-examination in Musk v. Altman, Brockman confirmed his OpenAI equity is worth ~$30 billion. Musk's lawyer revealed Brockman's journal passage asking 'financially what will take me to $1B?' and questioned why he didn't donate the additional $29B to the nonprofit. Brockman disclosed personal investments: Stripe ($471M), Cerebras ($2.8M), CoreWeave ($817K), Helion ($434K), Astral ($166K), and Scale AI.
cut_reason: Clustered with Musk v. Altman trial (TechCrunch as primary). Detail folded into cluster.

headline: Former Columbia Law Dean Testifies OpenAI Nonprofit Should Hold 'a Lot More' Than $200 Billion
raw_snippet: David Schizer, former dean of Columbia Law School, testified for Musk that OpenAI's reliance on Microsoft investment rather than charitable donations forced it to give up technology rights and future profits. He argued the charitable arm should hold 'a lot more' than the approximately $200 billion it currently has, suggesting the for-profit conversion shortchanged the nonprofit mission.
cut_reason: Clustered with Musk v. Altman trial (TechCrunch as primary). Detail folded into cluster.

headline: Court Filing Reveals Musk Sought Settlement with OpenAI Days Before Trial
raw_snippet: A court filing revealed Elon Musk messaged OpenAI President Greg Brockman on April 25 to 'gauge interest in settlement.' When Brockman suggested both sides drop claims, Musk replied: 'By the end of this week, you and Sam will be the most hated men in America.' Judge Gonzalez Rogers denied OpenAI's request to admit the filing as evidence. OpenAI argued the offer proved Musk's motive was to 'attack a competitor.'
cut_reason: Clustered with Musk v. Altman trial (TechCrunch as primary). Detail folded into cluster.

headline: Former OpenAI Employees Testify Altman's Character Failed Safety Standards at Trial
raw_snippet: Week 2 of the Musk v. Altman trial featured damaging testimony from former OpenAI colleagues about Sam Altman's leadership and the company's commitment to AI safety. OpenAI targets Musk's motives as the trial reaches its midpoint, arguing Musk's lawsuit is driven by competitive interests from his own AI company xAI rather than genuine concern for the nonprofit mission.
cut_reason: Clustered with Musk v. Altman trial (TechCrunch as primary). Detail folded into cluster.

headline: Musk v. Altman Trial Week 2: Former Safety Researcher Testifies (Local News Matters duplicate)
raw_snippet: In Day 8 of the Oakland federal trial, former AI safety researcher Rosie Campbell testified OpenAI gradually shifted to a product-focused approach, eliminated both long-term safety teams, and ~50% of her team departed rather than accept reassignment. A former board member testified Altman falsely claimed legal cleared GPT-4 Turbo for India launch without safety board review, causing recurring 'crisis events.'
cut_reason: Duplicate of Musk v. Altman trial coverage (TechCrunch as primary).

headline: Workspace Agents in ChatGPT Begin Credit-Based Pricing
raw_snippet: OpenAI's workspace agents for ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, and Edu plans transitioned from free preview to credit-based pricing on May 6. Workspace agents are powered by Codex and can complete repeatable tasks across Slack, Salesforce, Google Drive, Notion, and Atlassian Rovo, running in the cloud even when the user is offline.
cut_reason: Minor pricing transition for existing feature; lower significance vs other Product items this week.

headline: OpenAI Introduces ChatGPT Futures: Class of 2026 with $10K Grants
raw_snippet: OpenAI announced the inaugural ChatGPT Futures Class of 2026, recognizing 26 students and young builders using AI. Each member receives a $10,000 grant and access to frontier models to continue their work.
cut_reason: Minor community/CSR program; low significance for IB audience.
